calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-07-2002 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-07-2002 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Trial day Settlement Conference. Judge: Thomas J. Mellon, Jr.; Clerk: Dan MacDuff; Reporter: Mary J. Hinds; Bailiff: Louise Pierotti. Plaintiff appears with counsel, Luis G. Molina; defendant appears with counsel, Terrance Heath. After a settlement conference, the case settles as follows: Judgment of posession to plaintiff, stayed until 11/8/02. The parties reserve all claims against one another. Each to pay own attorney fees and costs. Neither side to harass or annoy the other; plaintiff agrees to co-operate with defendant when he comes to the premises to collect his belongings. If defendant vacates as agreed, plaintiff agrees to have this judgment vatated and to dismiss this case. Plaintiff further agrees to dismiss his Small Claims action against defendant. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare a judgment.

Notice Of Motion To Continue Trial
Off Calendar May-10-2005 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Continue Trial
Off Calendar Oct-03-2002 Continued To Oct-04-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION Notice Of Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-24-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William S. Weisberg, Esq. of WEISBERG & MILLER 296-7070, moving to be relieved as defendant's counsel
Steve Young Noh appeared in propria persona 577-5606, opposing party
* The Court conducted an in-camera hearing. William S. Weisberg was sworn and examined on his own behalf.
** Parties argued and the Court granted attorney WILLIAM STEVEN WEISBERG's motion to be relieved as counsel. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
*** The Court further ordered that Mr. Noh to pick up his file from Mr. Weisberg's office at noon tomorrow. Transcript of the in-camera hearing was ordered sealed.(JJM/301) Court Reporter Eric Throne, CSR 7855

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William S. Weisberg, Esq., WEISBERG & MILLER 296-7070, for defendant Steve Young Noh moving for summary judgment
Luis Gustavo Molina, Esq., 647-6003, for plaintiff Frank Yao and opposing party
* A R G U E D and the Court denied defendant STEVE YOUNG NOH's motion for summary judgment, triable issue of fact as to nuisance claims. prevailing is to prepare order. (JJM/301) Hon. JAMES J. McBRIDE, Court Reporter Eric Throne, CSR 7855

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-16-2002 Continued To Oct-07-02 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2002 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause comes on regular calendar this day for settlement conference. Plaintiff appearing by and through counsel, Luis Gustavo Molina, Esq., William Steven Weisberg, Esq., appeared for the defendant. No settlement reached. Trial date is confirmed.
